Title: Genome-wide transcriptomic and proteomic analyses of bollworm-infested developing cotton bolls revealed the genes and pathways involved in the insect pest defence mechanism

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes alternative respiration and participates in mitochondrial retrograde signalling during bollworm defence response
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOX transcripts were consistently induced by bollworm infestation. 2. AOX was highlighted as a component of mitochondrial retrograde signalling. 3. AOX induction indicates activation of alternative respiratory pathways during defence.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a retrograde signalling marker rather than direct nutrient metabolite biosynthetic gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chloroplast retrograde signalling associated with defence reprogramming
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GUN1 transcripts were strongly induced in bollworm-infested bolls. 2. The authors curated GUN1 as a chloroplast retrograde signalling component. 3. This supports organelle-driven nuclear reprogramming favoring defence over growth.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a biosynthetic enzyme or TF; classified here as common gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with activated jasmonate signalling during bollworm infestation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ1 was up-regulated upon bollworm attack. 2. This provides additional evidence of activated JA signalling. 3. It was included among crucial hormone-related genes in the results table.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although JAZ1 is a regulatory protein, the paper does not functionally validate it as a primary regulator; included as common gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes trehalose-6-phosphate from sugar precursors in trehalose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes trehalose biosynthesis associated with defence-related metabolic reprogramming under bollworm infestation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmoprotection and stabilization of proteins/macromolecular structures during stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in bollworm-infested bolls, especially at 5 and 10 dpa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS transcripts were significantly up-regulated in bollworm-infested cotton bolls. 2. TPS was highlighted as a key enzyme for trehalose biosynthesis during defence reprogramming. 3. Increased TPS expression supports accumulation of compatible solutes favoring defence over growth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May contribute to defence-growth tradeoff through metabolic resource reallocation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers metabolite accumulation from pathway expression patterns rather than direct trehalose quantification.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes trehalose accumulation-associated defence response under bollworm infestation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmoprotection and macromolecular stabilization during stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated at later stages, especially 5 and 10 dpa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPP transcript was induced in bollworm-infested bolls. 2. TPP completes trehalose biosynthesis together with TPS. 3. Its induction supports stress-associated accumulation of compatible solutes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible defence-growth tradeoff due to carbon repartitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal metabolite inference is based on pathway curation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes galactinol used for raffinose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes raffinose-related osmoprotectant biosynthesis during bollworm attack
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Two isoforms consistently up-regulated across stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in infested bolls at multiple developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two GolS isoforms were consistently up-regulated in infested bolls. 2. GolS was manually curated as contributing to raffinose biosynthesis. 3. Induction suggests increased osmoprotectant production during insect stress.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Carbon diversion to osmoprotectants may accompany reduced growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Table 2 notes HSf-1 as regulator of GolS.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes malate biosynthesis associated with stress response in infested bolls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress-associated metabolite implicated in defence-related metabolic adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated, especially at 5 and 10 dpa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Malate synthase transcript was strongly induced by bollworm infestation. 2. The expression pattern correlated with inferred stress-responsive malate accumulation. 3. This supports metabolic reallocation toward stress-adaptive metabolites.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes oxidation of malate in central carbon metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced MDH expression is associated with maintenance of malate accumulation under stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress-associated metabolite implicated in defence-related metabolic adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated at transcript and protein levels under infestation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics (GEO: GSE55511); proteomics by 2D PAGE/MALDI TOF/TOF
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MDH transcripts and proteins were down-regulated during infestation. 2. This pattern contrasted with induction of malate synthase. 3. The authors linked this shift to stress-responsive malate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect central carbon metabolism and respiration.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrogen remobilization and stress-associated amino acid metabolism in infested bolls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nitrogen remobilization supporting stress response and senescence-associated regulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Consistently up-regulated; stress-specific isoform noted in proteome
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptomics (GEO: GSE55511); proteomics by 2D PAGE/MALDI TOF/TOF
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GS1 was consistently induced under bollworm stress. 2. The authors interpreted GS1 induction as evidence of organic nitrogen remobilization rather than growth-associated primary nitrogen assimilation. 3. A stress-specific GS1 protein isoform was detected.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Association with senescence-related remobilization may negatively affect growth.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes interconversion of glutamate and 2-oxoglutarate with ammonium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports stress-associated nitrogen remobilization during bollworm infestation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GDH was induced by bollworm infestation. 2. Together with GS1 and NiR, GDH supported a stress-responsive nitrogen remobilization pattern. 3. This metabolic shift was interpreted as favouring defence over growth.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could reinforce senescence/remobilization at expense of growth.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to altered nitrogen metabolism during defence response
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrogen mobilization for defence metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated, especially at later stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NiR transcripts were up-regulated during infestation. 2. NiR was grouped with GS1 and GDH in altered nitrogen metabolism. 3. The pattern indicated defence-associated nitrogen reprogramming.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning nitrogen flux under biotic stress.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect growth-related nitrogen assimilation balance.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis during bollworm-induced defence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOS transcripts were up-regulated under bollworm infestation. 2. AOS was highlighted among crucial JA biosynthesis enzymes. 3. Its induction supports activation of JA-dependent defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime candidate for enhancing insect-induced JA signalling in cotton.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced JA signalling may reduce growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Localization is inferred from canonical JA pathway enzymes, not directly tested in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis during insect stress
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics by 2D PAGE/MALDI TOF/TOF; transcriptome context from pathway curation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: proteomics; qRT-PCR validation mentioned for selected genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC was identified among induced JA-pathway components. 2. Its expression supported activation of the JA branch under bollworm attack. 3. JA induction was interpreted as synergistic with ethylene and antagonistic to SA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target to reinforce JA-mediated insect defence.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible growth suppression due to elevated defence hormone flux.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Listed as a crucial phytohormone biosynthesis gene in discussion and Figure 6.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene biosynthesis during bollworm-induced defence response
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress and defence signalling; cooperates with jasmonate during insect response
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACS6 was consistently induced by bollworm infestation. 2. The study highlighted ACS as a crucial enzyme for ethylene biosynthesis. 3. Ethylene was inferred to act synergistically with JA in defence.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering insect-responsive ethylene signalling, potentially in combination with JA-pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ethylene activation may inhibit growth and affect development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subcellular location inferred from known ACS biology, not directly tested.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene formation during bollworm-induced defence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stress and defence signalling; cooperates with jasmonate during insect response
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACO4 was induced in bollworm-infested bolls. 2. ACO4 complements ACS induction to support ethylene production. 3. The ET pathway was interpreted as synergistically activated with JA.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in engineering elevated inducible ethylene-mediated defence, possibly together with ACS.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA precursor formation during insect attack
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Helicoverpa armigera infestation under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX2 and LOX3 transcripts were up-regulated during infestation. 2. They were part of the induced JA biosynthesis module. 3. Their activation supports lipid precursor flux toward defence hormone production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing upstream JA flux in insect defence engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert lipid metabolism away from growth-associated pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation in retrograde stress signalling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/associates with AOX expression in mitochondrial retrograde signalling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AOX and nuclear stress-response genes associated with mitochondrial retrograde signalling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: retrograde signalling and defence-associated metabolic reprogramming
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking mitochondrial stress signalling to defence metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes defence-associated retrograde signalling under bollworm infestation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in infested bolls across stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY40 was strongly induced by bollworm infestation. 2. The authors curated WRKY40 as a component of mitochondrial retrograde signalling. 3. WRKY40 is discussed as regulating AOX expression during stress.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory target to reinforce mitochondrial stress signalling and insect defence.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could enhance defence at the expense of growth-related programmes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOX regulation is supported by literature/manual curation cited in discussion, not by direct binding assay in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation; suppressor of salicylic acid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts as suppressor of SA-related responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phytohormone crosstalk and defence branch selection
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Helps shift signalling away from antagonistic SA toward JA-ethylene defence
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses antagonistic SA signalling and supports JA/ethylene-biased defence response
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: biotic stress signalling, often antagonistic to JA in this context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 was up-regulated in bollworm-infested bolls. 2. The authors identified WRKY33 as a suppressor of salicylic acid. 3. This supports preferential activation of synergistic JA–ethylene signalling over antagonistic SA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering hormone branch selection favoring insect defence.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May weaken SA-dependent resistance traits against other pathogens.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suppression of SA is inferred from pathway curation and prior knowledge cited by authors.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation in ethylene signalling; suppressor of salicylic acid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ethylene-responsive defence outputs and suppresses SA-related responses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phytohormone signalling crosstalk and defence regulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ethylene-pathway regulator contributing to JA-ET cooperative defence state
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ethylene-mediated defence and suppresses antagonistic SA pathway under infestation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN3 transcripts were up-regulated in infested bolls. 2. The authors identified EIN3 as a suppressor of salicylic acid signalling. 3. EIN3 supports the JA–ethylene defence program during bollworm attack.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for manipulating ethylene-driven insect defence, especially together with ACS/ACO or JA-pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter defence balance against pathogens requiring SA signalling; growth effects possible.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in Table 2 and Figure 6 as part of suppressors of SA.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Links stress signalling to osmoprotectant biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes galactinol/raffinose-related defence metabolism during bollworm infestation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HSf-1 was induced by bollworm infestation. 2. Table 2 identifies HSf-1 as a regulator of GolS. 3. This implies a regulatory link between stress TF activation and raffinose-family osmoprotectant biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream regulator to coordinately enhance osmoprotectant metabolism in stress-tolerant cotton.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader heat-shock/stress transcriptional effects may produce pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not provide direct promoter-binding validation.
